bio.quote: Brynja Steinsdóttir is a broad-shouldered woman with calloused hands and the steady voice of someone who has been making decisions for forty people and hasn't slept in days. Before the Burning worsene

system.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

novice.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

system.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

system.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

novice.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

system.description: Advantage (Second Chance) on Endurance tests. Your *slowed* speed is 3 instead of 2.

novice.description: Advantage (Second Chance) on Endurance tests. Your *slowed* speed is 3 instead of 2.

system.description: When you take the Help action, the bonus granted is +2 instead of +1.

novice.description: When you take the Help action, the bonus granted is +2 instead of +1.

system.description:
A chartered frontier company that works the ash-woods and burned margins: disciplined patrols, quotas, and the grim etiquette of boundary law.

Language: Askeskogsmål

Environment: Wilderness; Organization: Bureaucratic; Upbringing: Martial

novice.description:

Language: Askeskogsmål

system.description:
Skills: Cunning, Vigilant, Streetwise.

Languages: One additional language.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 150

Perk: *Paper Shield*. Once per session, you can produce documentation (real or plausible) that grants an advantage (second chance) on one social test made in a lawful setting.

novice.description:

Languages: One additional language.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 150

Perk: *Paper Shield*. Once per session, you can produce documentation (real or plausible) that grants an advantage (second chance) on one social test made in a lawful setting.

system.description: Whenever you make a test that uses the Brag, Flirt, or Intimidate skills, you can use Might instead of any other characteristic the test calls for.

novice.description: Whenever you make a test that uses the Brag, Flirt, or Intimidate skills, you can use Might instead of any other characteristic the test calls for.

system.description:
Strategist. Defender. Leader. With weapon in hand, you lead allies into the maw of battle, barking out commands that inspire your fellow heroes to move faster and strike more precisely. All the while, you stand between your compatriots and death, taunting the followers of evil to best you if they can.

As a tactician, you have abilities that heal your allies and grant them increased damage, movement, and attacks, even as you leave your enemies struggling to respond.

novice.description:

system.description:
You have drilled with a broad array of arms and armor, and have developed techniques to optimize their use. You can use and gain the benefits of two kits, including both their signature abilities. Whenever you would choose or change one kit, you can choose or change your second kit as well. See Chapter 6: Kits for more information. (Quick Build: Shining Armor, Sniper.)

If both kits grant you the same benefit, you take one or the other and can’t change your choice until you finish a extended rest. (This usually means taking the higher of two bonuses.)

For example, if you take the Shining Armor and Sniper kits, you gain the following benefits overall:

  • Toughness Bonus: +12 per echelon

  • Resistance Bonus: +1

  • Melee Damage Bonus: +2/+2/+2

  • Ranged Damage Bonus: +0/+0/+4

  • Speed Bonus: +1

  • Ranged Distance Bonus: +10

  • Disengage Bonus: +1

  • You can use the Patient Shot and Protective Attack signature abilities.

Kit signature abilities have their kit’s bonuses already applied, which might require you to adjust the bonuses of the signature abilities you gain from a kit. For example, you might take the Martial Artist kit, which gives a melee weapon damage bonus of +2/+2/+2, and the Mountain kit, which gives a melee weapon damage bonus of +0/+0/+4. If you choose to use the Mountain kit’s damage bonus, then the Battle Grace signature ability from the Martial Artist kit loses the +2/+2/+2 bonus from that kit, reducing its usual 5/8/11 damage for its tier 1, tier 2, and tier 3 outcomes to 3/6/9. It then gains the +0/+0/+4 of the Mountain kit to deal 3/6/13 damage.

system.description: The retiarius is often depicted as a lightly armored warrior with a net in one hand and a trident in the other, and this kit gives you the equipment and fighting technique to make that happen. Tie up your foe with a net and then poke them to death!

novice.description: The retiarius is often depicted as a lightly armored warrior with a net in one hand and a trident in the other, and this kit gives you the equipment and fighting technique to make that happen. Tie up your foe with a net and then poke them to death!

system.description: The Whirlwind kit makes effective use of whips, granting you mobility, damage, and reach. If you want to be a fast-moving warrior who lashes foes with a chain or whip, then this is the kit for you

novice.description: The Whirlwind kit makes effective use of whips, granting you mobility, damage, and reach. If you want to be a fast-moving warrior who lashes foes with a chain or whip, then this is the kit for you

system.description:
The ring of steel panics others but brings order to your mind, granting you a Heroic Resource called focus.

Focus in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain focus equal to your Milestones. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 2 focus.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that you or any ally damages a creature marked by you (see @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.zyd6C7phOZr6EAQ9]{Mark}), you gain 1 focus. The first time in a combat round that any ally within 10 meters of you uses a heroic ability, you gain 1 focus.

You lose any remaining focus at the end of the encounter.

Focus Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain focus outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost focus without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs focus, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a extended rest.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited focus on its effect, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of focus equal to your Milestones.

system.description: Even before battle begins, your enemies struggle to keep up with your tactics. At the start of an encounter, you can use a free triggered action to use your Mark ability against one enemy you have line of effect to, even if you are surprised. You can then slide the marked target up to 3 meters, ignoring resistance. The target can’t be moved in a way that would harm them (such as over a cliff), leave them dying, or result in them suffering a condition or other negative effect.

novice.description: Even before battle begins, your enemies struggle to keep up with your tactics. system.description: You can don or remove a disguise as part of any test you make using the Hide skill, or while using the Hide free action.

novice.description: You can don or remove a disguise as part of any test you make using the Hide skill, or while using the Hide free action.

system.description: You have learned the stratagems of ancient heroes, letting you lead from the front lines and seek victory through sheer force of will and personality. You gain a skill from the interpersonal skill group.

novice.description: You have learned the stratagems of ancient heroes, letting you lead from the front lines and seek victory through sheer force of will and personality. You gain a skill from the interpersonal skill group.

system.description: You command any room you walk into. While you are present during a negotiation, each hero with you treats their Renown as 2 higher than usual. Additionally, each hero with you during a combat encounter has a double advantage (second chance) on tests made to stop combat and start a negotiation.

novice.description: You command any room you walk into. While you are present during a negotiation, each hero with you treats their Renown as 2 higher than usual. Additionally, each hero with you during a combat encounter has a double advantage (second chance) on tests made to stop combat and start a negotiation.

system.description:
After constant drills, you can more accurately anticipate an enemy’s plan and thwart their attempts to move across the battlefield. Whenever you make an free attack, the target’s speed is reduced to 0 until the end of the current turn.

Mark Benefit: When a creature marked by you attempts to move or shift within distance of your melee Free Attack, you can use a free triggered action and spend 2 focus to make a melee Free Attack against that creature
